Scenario.title: Operation MidnightEclipse: Targeting Transportation Systems
Scenario.overview: Transportation Systems Simple A sophisticated attack targeting transportation infrastructure leverages a zero-day vulnerability to compromise network controls and disrupt operations through denial-of-service mechanisms.
Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Internet-facing assets, Malicious Payload, Network Traffic
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: General Access Method, Command and Scripting Interpreter, Network Traffic Content (Mechanism)

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker exploits a zero-day vulnerability (CVE-2024-3400) in GlobalProtect, a public-facing application, to gain unauthorized access. This exploit leverages a misconfigured test interface to bypass authentication and establish a foothold in the network.

stage.name: Execution
stage.description: Using the initial access, the attacker executes arbitrary commands via a command injection flaw in the compromised application. This allows them to deploy a payload that establishes persistent control over the targeted system.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker overwhelms network infrastructure with malformed packets or corrupts critical traffic streams, causing denial-of-service conditions. This disrupts transportation systems by crippling network controls and preventing operational continuity.

Scenario.summary: Operation MidnightEclipse exploits a zero-day vulnerability in internet-facing applications to execute arbitrary commands and trigger denial-of-service attacks, targeting transportation networks to induce operational disruption.
